text.title: Exploratory Charter Examples
text.summary: Worked examples — one structured session and one antipattern caught by the validator.

example.title: Onboarding-email i18n exploration session
example.note: Charter ES-014: 75 min, mission "explore email-onboarding to discover failure modes in i18n, timezone, rendering". Focus areas: i18n input, timezone display, email-provider rendering. Heuristics: input variety, SFDPOT. Observation log captures 17 entries over 72 minutes including one bug (#2118 ZWJ emoji not rendered) and two follow-ups (does support pipeline share the rendering layer; API sanitiser weaker than UI). Debrief published at +5 hours: three themes (i18n bugs cluster in rendering, API weaker than UI, datetime format inconsistent), one bug, one follow-up, one debt item. Validator: OK.

example.title: Scripted-in-disguise session rejected
example.note: Charter ES-022: 90 min, mission "test the billing flow". Focus areas list contains 14 line items resembling a checklist ("click Subscribe; verify success page; click Cancel; verify cancel"). Observation log has 22 entries — most are tick-tick-tick on the checklist. Validator rejects on f6 (scope-creep, focus_areas > 5) and on f1 (mission too short / vague). Explorer rewrites the charter with a single mission ("explore the cancel-and-resubscribe loop for state-leak bugs") and 4 focus areas; second session yields three real findings.
